pi_items.source: Mathematical verification using formula: Candela = Lumens / [2π(1 - cos(θ/2))], confirmed by compuphase.com and rapidtables.com
Verified against verifier.py tolerance_pct parameter and calculation range

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic mathematical calculation task using the standard photometry formula Candela = Lumens / [2π(1 - cos(θ/2))]. With inputs of 4500 lumens and 36-degree beam angle, the mathematically correct result is 14633.15 candelas, which rounds to 14633. The metadata claims an expected value of 14634, which differs by 1 unit (0.006% error) from the correct rounded value. The tolerance of 5% is reasonable for this calculation. All file paths and configuration parameters are correctly specified in the task setup and export scripts.
